testcases.input: a = "aba", b = "cdc" Output: 3 Explanation: One longest uncommon subsequence is "aba" because "aba" is a subsequence of "aba" but not "cdc". Note that "cdc" is also a longest uncommon subsequence.
testcases.expectedOutput: 3 Explanation: One longest uncommon subsequence is "aba" because "aba" is a subsequence of "aba" but not "cdc". Note that "cdc" is also a longest uncommon subsequence.

testcases.input: a = "aaa", b = "bbb" Output: 3 Explanation: The longest uncommon subsequences are "aaa" and "bbb".
testcases.expectedOutput: 3 Explanation: The longest uncommon subsequences are "aaa" and "bbb".

testcases.input: a = "aaa", b = "aaa" Output: -1 Explanation: Every subsequence of string a is also a subsequence of string b. Similarly, every subsequence of string b is also a subsequence of string a. So the answer would be -1.
testcases.expectedOutput: -1 Explanation: Every subsequence of string a is also a subsequence of string b. Similarly, every subsequence of string b is also a subsequence of string a. So the answer would be -1.
